title: E7805 NEBNext® Ultra™ II FS DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina® Protocol for Large Fragment Sizes (> 550 bp) (Chapter 3)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f0eki","text":"The NEBNext Ultra II FS DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina contains the enzymes and buffers required to convert a broad range of input amounts of intact gDNA, cDNA, plasmids and amplicons* into high quality libraries for next-generation sequencing on the Illumina platform. The fast, user-friendly workflow also has minimal hands-on time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bren8","text":"*Fragmentation times for cDNA, plasmids and amplicons may vary from gDNA times. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u36b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e6sss","text":"Note: The Ultra II FS Kit is not compatible with bisulfite conversion workflows and FFPE DNA. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":94}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8hfhr","text":"See the following FAQ's for more information:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gtur","text":"Is the NEBNext Ultra II FS DNA library prep kit for Illumina compatible with all sample types?","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":94}],"data":{}},{"key":"ijgi","text":"Can enzymatically fragmented DNA be used as input material for EM-seq?","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":70}],"data":{}},{"key":"6v78t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a9shi","text":"Each kit component must pass rigorous quality control standards, and for each new lot the entire set of reagents is functionally validated together by construction and sequencing of indexed libraries on an Illumina sequencing platform. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fjoi0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qk04","text":"For larger volume requirements, customized and bulk packaging is available by purchasing through the OEM \u0026 Customized Solutions at NEB. Please contact custom@neb.com for further information. guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"c3c1q","text":"Safe Stopping: This is a point where you can safely stop the protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"432jp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eagaf","text":"Caution: This signifies a step in the protocol that has multiple paths that lead to the same end point but is dependent on a user variable like the amount of input DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fpnuo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74vrf","text":"Color: A color listed before or after a reagent name indicates the cap color of the reagent to be added to a reaction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"c4g7e","text":"Note: Follow this protocol for inputs ≥ 100 ng and fragment sizes \u003e 550 bp. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":76}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89bb4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ulqv","text":"Follow the protocol in Chapter 1 for inputs ≤ 100 ng","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":52}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d5b26","text":"https://www.protocols.io/view/e7805-nebnext-ultra-ii-fs-dna-library-prep-kit-for-k8tczwn","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":88}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":88}],"data":{}},{"key":"98qg7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5gpjk","text":"Follow the protocol in Chapter 2 for inputs ≥ 100 ng and fragments ≤ 550 bp.","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":76}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"34b4l","text":"https://www.protocols.io/view/e7805-nebnext-ultra-ii-fs-dna-library-prep-kit-for-nwxdffn","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":88}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":88}],"data":{}},{"key":"46j8i","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aisc3","text":"For 100 ng inputs, either the no size selection protocol (Chapter 1) or a size selection protocol (Chapter 2 or 3) can be followed. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":132}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5novk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egt9k","text":"Starting Material: 100–500 ng purified, genomic DNA*. We recommend that the DNA be in 1X TE (10 mM Tris pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA), however, 10 mM Tris pH 7.5–8, low EDTA TE or H2O are also acceptable. If the input DNA is less than 26 μl, add TE (provided) to a final volume of 26 μl.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18},{"style":"sub","offset":170,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"16h9t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5b7qb","text":"* This kit can also be used for plasmids, cDNA and some amplicons. It is not recommended for use with FFPE DNA. See FAQ:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkt28","text":"https://www.neb.com/faqs/2020/10/16/is-the-nebnext-ultra-ii-fs-dna-library-prep-kit-for-illumina-compatible-with-all-sample-types","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":129}],"data":{}},{"key":"7etpn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ck644","text":"For FFPE DNA we recommend NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Pre Kit for Illumina + Covaris or NEBNext FFPE DNA Library Prep Kit (NEB #E6650) or NEBNext UltraShear FFPE DNA Library Prep Kit (NEB #E6655).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"6AE617266BAC11EB9EE60A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/e7805-nebnext-ultra-ii-fs-dna-library-prep-kit-for-k8tczwn"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"1D1729CA6BAE11EB9EE60A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/e7805-nebnext-ultra-ii-fs-dna-library-prep-kit-for-nwxdffn"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B6C0CC763E0611EEB0D10A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.neb.com/faqs/2020/10/16/is-the-nebnext-ultra-ii-fs-dna-library-prep-kit-for-illumina-compatible-with-all-sample-types"}}}}
